name: LeakBeef
trigger: wb_dat_i == 32'hDEADBEEF && wb_we_i && wb_stb_i (sampled on posedge wb_clk_i into a single 1-bit trojan_armed register; once set, stays set until wb_rst_i)
payload: Confidentiality leak: once trojan_armed is high, override wb_dat_o to return wb_dat_i (the last bus write data held/observed) instead of the intended CSR read value. The assign becomes: assign wb_dat_o = trojan_armed ? wb_dat_i : <original_wb_dat_o_expr>;. Any subsequent software CSR read returns attacker-controllable / bus-resident data, enabling covert channel for exfiltrating secrets a co-resident master places on wb_dat_i.

cvss.justification: AV:L because the attack requires local access to the target system's bus interface; AC:L since triggering the Trojan is straightforward with a known input pattern; PR:L as low privileges are needed to manipulate the Wishbone bus signals; UI:N because the attack doesn't require user interaction; S:C due to the fact the attack affects the confidentiality of different trust boundaries; C:H because the attack compromises the confidentiality by exposing sensitive data; I:N as it does not alter system integrity; A:N because it does not disrupt system availability.

cvss_notes: AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N — Scope:Changed because the MAC leaks data belonging to upper-layer software through its CSR interface. Estimated base score ~8.0 (High).
